paper_title: Holocene-Length Temperature Reconstructions from Lakes in Northwest Spitsbergen

methods.description: Selection of lake sites for temperature reconstruction based on climatic setting and previous studies, followed by sampling of new material from previously collected cores.

steps.description: Select sites Gjøavatnet, Hakluytvatnet, and Hajeren for their near-identical climatic setting and proximity to the sea ice margin.
steps.searchable_summary: select lake sites based on climatic setting and proximity to sea ice margin

steps.description: Sample new material from previously collected cores at the selected sites.
steps.searchable_summary: sample new material from existing cores

methods.method_name: Temperature Reconstruction Using Long Chain Alkenones
methods.description: Reconstruction of Holocene growing season surface temperature variability using Long Chain Alkenones (LCAs) unsaturation as a paleothermometer.

steps.description: Extract, saponify, separate, and measure LCAs from sediment samples.
steps.searchable_summary: process sediment samples to extract and measure LCAs

steps.description: Use the U K 37 index to resolve tri-unsaturated isomers for chemotaxonomic identification of the alkenone producer.
steps.searchable_summary: apply U K 37 index for chemotaxonomic identification

methods.description: Statistical analysis of temperature reconstructions and correlation with other regional temperature records.

steps.description: Calculate Pearson correlation between composite temperature record and individual lake reconstructions at 100-year intervals.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Pearson correlation analysis at 100-year intervals

steps.description: Compare composite stack to Kongressvatnet record for validation using observations.
steps.searchable_summary: validate composite stack against Kongressvatnet record
